description: If something is P, and all things that are P make B true, then B is true.

hints.text: You're given two [premise|premises]: there exists something that is $P$, and everything that is $P$ [implication|implies] $B$. The [conclusion|conclusion] is $B$. The strategy: open the existential [witness|witness], use the universal to get $P(witness) \to B$, then close. Assume both premises to start.
Assume the existential: there is some $P$.
Assume the universal: everything $P$ leads to $B$.
Instantiate the universal with the [witness|witness] term $y$ using `forall-elim`.
Merge $y$ with $P(y)$ using `and-intro` to bring them into one [context|context].

Close the [witness|witness] with `exists-elim` — the existential (step 1), the witness subproof (step 8), and the witness variable $y$. The engine [assumption|discharges] both the witness [atom|atom] and its instantiated body.
